appearflag_singleplayer.title: #AppearFlags SinglePlayer
appearflag_singleplayer.description: Appearance flags for SinglePlayer, This control when this entity should or should not appear in this condition.

-1.title: Not in
-1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is SinglePlayer.

0.title: Default
0.description: This entity is not affected by the game being SinglePlayer

1.title: Only in
1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is NOT SinglePlayer

appearflag_multiplayer.title: #AppearFlags MultiPlayer
appearflag_multiplayer.description: Appearance flags for MultiPlayer, This control when this entity should or should not appear in this condition.

-1.title: Not in
-1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is MultiPlayer.

0.title: Default
0.description: This entity is not affected by the game being MultiPlayer

1.title: Only in
1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is NOT MultiPlayer

appearflag_cooperative.title: #AppearFlags Cooperative
appearflag_cooperative.description: Appearance flags for Cooperative, This control when this entity should or should not appear in this condition.

-1.title: Not in
-1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is Cooperative.

0.title: Default
0.description: This entity is not affected by the game being Cooperative

1.title: Only in
1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is NOT Cooperative

appearflag_skilleasy.title: #AppearFlags Easy
appearflag_skilleasy.description: Appearance flags for Skill level Easy, This control when this entity should or should not appear in this condition.

-1.title: Not in
-1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is Easy.

0.title: Default
0.description: This entity is not affected by the game being Easy

1.title: Only in
1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is NOT Easy

appearflag_skillmedium.title: #AppearFlags Medium
appearflag_skillmedium.description: Appearance flags for Skill level Medium, This control when this entity should or should not appear in this condition.

-1.title: Not in
-1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is Medium.

0.title: Default
0.description: This entity is not affected by the game being Medium

1.title: Only in
1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is NOT Medium

appearflag_skillhard.title: #AppearFlags Hard
appearflag_skillhard.description: Appearance flags for Skill level Hard, This control when this entity should or should not appear in this condition.

-1.title: Not in
-1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is Hard.

0.title: Default
0.description: This entity is not affected by the game being Hard

1.title: Only in
1.description: This entity won't exist if the game is NOT Hard

message.title: Path/filename.wav of WAV
message.description: This defines which sound file will be played by the entity. This accepts an input of the form path/filename.wav (starting from the 'sound' folder). (Example: ambience/drips.wav). The name of a sentence defined in sentences.txt will also be accepted with the form '!SENTENCENAME'. (Example: !HG_ALERT1)

volstart.title: Start Volume
volstart.description: Sets the volume that the sound should start at when fading in, and that it should stop at when fading out. Scales from 0 (completely silent) to 10 (maximum). (Should be lower than Volume.)

fadein.title: Fade in time (0-100)
fadein.description: Sets how long the sound should take to fade in from Start Volume to Volume. Scales from 0 (instant) to 100 (very long).

fadeout.title: Fade out time (0-100)
fadeout.description: Sets how long the sound should take to fade out from Volume to Start Volume. Scales from 0 (instant) to 100 (very long).

pitch.title: Pitch (> 100 = higher)
pitch.description: Sets the pitch of the sound. Scales from 100 (default), with lower values meaning lower pitch and higher values meaning higher pitch.

pitchstart.title: Start Pitch
pitchstart.description: Sets the pitch that the sound should start at when spinning up, and that it should stop at when spinning down. Scales from 100 (default), with lower values meaning lower pitch and higher values meaning higher pitch. (Should be lower than Pitch.)

spinup.title: Spin up time (0-100)
spinup.description: Sets how long the sound should take to spin up from Start Pitch to Pitch. Scales from 0 (instant) to 100 (very long).

spindown.title: Spin down time (0-100)
spindown.description: Sets how long the sound should take to spin down from Pitch to Start Pitch. Scales from 0 (instant) to 100 (very long).

lfotype.title: LFO type
lfotype.description: Allows the sound to have certain oscillating effects applied to its pitch.

1.title: sqr
1.description: Switches between a low and high pitch.

2.title: tri
2.description: Oscillates between a low and high pitch.

lforate.title: LFO rate (0-1000)
lforate.description: Sets how quickly the pitch changes with the LFO effects. Scales between 0 (does not change) and 1000 (changes extremely quickly).

lfomodpitch.title: LFO mod pitch (0-100)
lfomodpitch.description: Sets how much the pitch should change with the LFO effects. Scales between 0 (does not change) and 100 (very large changes).

lfomodvol.title: LFO mod vol (0-100)
lfomodvol.description: Sets how much the volume should change with the LFO effects. Seems to coincide with lower pitches. Scales between 0 (does not change) and 100 (silent).

cspinup.title: Incremental spinup count
cspinup.description: Sets how many times the ambient_generic can be triggered to have the pitch spin up on top of the pitch it was already playing at. Setting this to a value other than 0 will disable toggling the sound, and will prevent the sound from being turned off even it has been triggered that many times.

1.title: Play Everywhere
1.description: If enabled, the sound will be heard throughout the entire map.

2.title: Small Radius
2.description: If enabled, the sound will be at maximum volume within a roughly 256 unit radius, fading out completely at around 512 units away.

4.title: Medium Radius
4.description: If enabled, the sound will be at maximum volume within a roughly 512 unit radius, fading out completely at around 768 units away.

8.title: Large Radius
8.description: If enabled, the sound will be at maximum volume within a roughly 768 unit radius, fading out completely at around 1280 units away.

16.title: Start Silent
16.description: If enabled, the sound will not play at the start of the map, and will need to be triggered to play. Otherwise, the sound will play at the start of the map whether it is a looping sound or not.

32.title: Is NOT Looped
32.description: Tells the entity whether or not it is dealing with a looping sound. If enabled, the sound will play whenever the entity is triggered (intended for non-looping sounds). (Looping sounds will still play, but will not turn off when triggered.) If not enabled, the sound will toggle on or off when triggered (intended for looping sounds). (Non-looping sounds will still play, but every second trigger will turn the sound off regardless of whether the sound is actually playing anything.) See notes for more information about looping sounds.

preset.title: Dynamic Presets
preset.description: Sets the ambient_generic to use a predefined set of dynamics. If set to anything other than 'None', this will override every other attribute apart from Name and WAV Name.

0.title: None
0.description: Does not use a preset, and will not override any other entity attributes.

1.title: Huge Machine
1.description: Very long spin up, starting slow and getting fast. Very long spin down.

2.title: Big Machine
2.description: Medium spin up, starting normal and getting fast. Long spin down.

3.title: Machine
3.description: Quick spin up, starting normal and getting fast. Quick spin down.

7.title: Slow Pulse
7.description: Slow pulsing speed. Quick fade out.

9.title: Quick pulse
9.description: Quick pulsing speed. Quick fade out.

10.title: Slow Oscillator
10.description: Slow oscillation between normal and slow speed. Quick fade out.

11.title: Oscillator
11.description: Medium oscillation between normal and slow speed. Quick fade out.

12.title: Quick Oscillator
12.description: Fast oscillation between normal and slow speed. Quick fade out.

19.title: Oscillate spinup/down
19.description: Medium oscillation, with long spin up from slow to fast. Long spin down.

20.title: Pulse spinup/down
20.description: Slow pulse, with long spin up from slow to fast. Long spin down.

21.title: Random pitch
21.description: Occasionally switches speed, tending towards slower speeds. Quick fade out.

22.title: Random pitch fast
22.description: Rapidly switches speed, tending towards faster speeds. Quick fade out.

23.title: Incremental Spinup
23.description: Quick spin up. Subsequent triggers will not toggle, but will spin up from existing speed, up to five times. Cannot be turned off.

24.title: Alien
24.description: Unusual pulsing and switching speed. Quick fade out.

25.title: Bizzare
25.description: Fast oscillation between fast and slow/silent. Quick fade out.

26.title: Planet X
26.description: Medium switching between slower speeds, similar to 'Random pitch'. Quick fade out.

27.title: Haunted
27.description: Slow switch between normal and slow speed. Quick fade out.

filename.title: Filename of music file
filename.description: Relative name of the file to play, starting in the root directory. For example: media/Half-Life01.mp3. Only applies to the Play and Loop commands

target_selector.title: Target selector
target_selector.description: How to select players to affect.

radius.title: Player radius (for Radius target selector)
radius.description: If target_selector is Radius, this is how close the player has to be to be affected by this entity

remove_on_fire.title: Remove on fire
remove_on_fire.description: Whether to remove the entity after it has been triggered or after it has affected at least one player (Radius only)

ammo_name.title: Ammo Name (Required)
ammo_name.description: Name of the ammo type to give.

ammo_amount.title: Ammo Amount To Give (-1 == Maximum)
ammo_amount.description: If set, the player will receive this much ammo instead of the default amount. If set to **-1** the player will receive the maximum amount of ammo for this type. Can be **0** to make a fake ammo entity.

respawn_delay.title: Respawn delay (-1 == Never)
respawn_delay.description: Custom respawn delay. If set, overrides the delay configured by the skill configuration. A delay of **-1** disables respawn. This allows items to respawn in singleplayer.

0.title: No
0.description: The items are invisible until respawn.

1.title: Yes
1.description: The item remains visible during the respawn time.

1.title: Yes
1.description: The item will flash when it respawns.

0.title: No
0.description: The item won't play its pickup sound.

1.title: Yes
1.description: The item will play its pickup sound.

fall_mode.title: Fall Mode
fall_mode.description: Controls whether the item falls to the ground or floats when it spawns.

0.title: Fall
0.description: Item falls to ground when it spawns

1.title: Float
1.description: Item floats in the air where it spawns

trigger_on_spawn.title: Trigger On Spawn
trigger_on_spawn.description: Target to trigger when the item (re)spawns.

trigger_on_despawn.title: Trigger On Despawn
trigger_on_despawn.description: Target to trigger when the item despawns. This happens when the player picks up the item.

4.title: Repeatable
4.description: If enabled, the sequence can be repeated more than once. Otherwise the entity will be removed once the sequence is complete.

8.title: Leave Corpse
8.description: If enabled, the monster leave it's corpse instead of fading out. This is only noticeable in-between two aiscripted_sequence when the monster is on a dying state.

m_iszEntity.title: Target Monster
m_iszEntity.description: The name of the monster entity that this sequence affects. You can also input a monster classname (monster_scientist, for example). (Only one monster will be chosen to follow the sequence.)

m_iszPlay.title: Action Animation
m_iszPlay.description: The name of the animation that will be performed by "Target Monster".

m_flRadius.title: Search Radius
m_flRadius.description: If you input a monster classname into "Target Monster", the entity will pick a monster within this radius of the entity to follow the sequence. If none are within the radius, the first monster to enter the radius will follow the sequence.

m_flRepeat.title: Repeat Rate ms
m_flRepeat.description: How often to check if a monster is within the search radius. A check will be made even if search radius is zero, so set this to a large value if it's not used.

m_fMoveTo.title: Move to Position
m_fMoveTo.description: Sets how (or if) the monster moves before performing the animation

0.title: No
0.description: The monster will not move or turn. It will perform the animation wherever it is.

1.title: Walk
1.description: The monster will walk to the aiscripted_sequence, then perform the animation.

2.title: Run
2.description: The monster will run to the aiscripted_sequence, then perform the animation.

4.title: Instantaneous
4.description: The monster will instantly warp to the location of the aiscripted_sequence and perform the animation.

5.title: No - Turn to Face
5.description: The monster will not move, but will turn to the aiscripted_sequence's angle before performing the animation.

m_iFinishSchedule.title: AI Schedule when done
m_iFinishSchedule.description: Defines what happens to the monster's AI when the sequence is completed/canceled.

0.title: Default AI
0.description: The AI will order the monster to clear it's schedule (kinda like a "reset"). It will resume normal behavior afterwards.

1.title: Ambush
1.description: The AI will order the monster to go into an "ambush" schedule. That is to wait for an infinite period of time unless it see an enemy or take damage.

model_replacement_filename.title: Model replacement file
model_replacement_filename.description: Path to the model replacement file. Must be a relative path starting in the mod directory. All search paths are checked, See Replacement Map System

sentence_replacement_filename.title: Sentence replacement file
sentence_replacement_filename.description: Path to the Sentence replacement file. Must be a relative path starting in the mod directory. All search paths are checked, See Replacement Map System

sound_replacement_filename.title: Sound replacement file
sound_replacement_filename.description: Path to the Sound replacement file. Must be a relative path starting in the mod directory. All search paths are checked, See Replacement Map System

texture.title: Sprite Name
texture.description: This defines which sprite file the entity will use to form the beam. This accepts an input of the form "path/filename.spr" (starting from the root folder). (Example: "sprites/laserbeam.spr").

LightningStart.title: Start Entity
LightningStart.description: The name of an entity to be used as the starting point for the beam. If undefined, the beam will choose a surface within the Radius to use as the starting point. (Note that if the Start Entity is undefined, the Ending Entity must also be undefined.)

LightningEnd.title: Ending Entity
LightningEnd.description: The name of an entity to be used as the end point for the beam. If undefined, the beam will choose a surface within the Radius to use as the end point.

Radius.title: Radius
Radius.description: If the beam has undefined start/end points, this attribute defines how far away the beam can choose to use as its start/end points.

life.title: Life (seconds 0 = infinite)
life.description: Sets how many seconds the beam will stay on before deactivating. It will reactivate through Strike again time. Setting this to 0 will make the beam never deactivate automatically.

BoltWidth.title: Width of beam (pixels*0.1 0-255)
BoltWidth.description: Sets the width of the beam. Measured in tenths of a unit. This attribute works like a radius; the beam will actually be twice as wide as the defined value.

NoiseAmplitude.title: Amount of noise (0-255)
NoiseAmplitude.description: Sets how erratic the beam will be. The beam will always start and end at the usual points, but the path of the beam will move randomly to a degree defined by this attribute. Scales from 0 (perfectly straight beam) to 255 (wildly moving beam).

TextureScroll.title: Texture Scroll Rate (0-100)
TextureScroll.description: Sets how fast the sprites move along the beam. Scales from 0 (no movement) to 100 (extremely fast).

framestart.title: Starting Frame
framestart.description: Sets which frame of animation that the sprite should use.

StrikeTime.title: Strike again time (secs)
StrikeTime.description: Sets how long many seconds before the beam reactivates (after the beam deactivates after being on for the time defined in Life). If the beam has undefined start/end points, the beam will choose new points for the reactivation.

damage.title: Damage / second
damage.description: Sets how much damage the beam should do when you walk through it. Note that the damage is only dealt in a straight line between start and end point, regardless of any fluctuation in the beam from the Amount of Noise attribute.

1.title: Start On
1.description: If enabled, the beam will be turned on upon map start. Otherwise you will need to trigger the env_beam to turn it on.

2.title: Toggle
2.description: If enabled, the beam can be retriggered to stop the beam from reactivating automatically. Otherwise, the beam seems to loop indefinitely regardless of further triggerings.

4.title: Random Strike
4.description: If enabled, the beam will vary its activation and deactivation times. These times will be scaled somewhat around the times you provide in Life and Strike Again Time.

8.title: Ring
8.description: If enabled, the beam will form a ring between its two points rather than a straight line. The ring will tend towards being horizontal, and may malform if the two points are in a perfectly vertical line.

16.title: StartSparks
16.description: If enabled, the start of the beam will spark when it is activated.

32.title: EndSparks
32.description: If enabled, the end of the beam will spark when it is activated.

128.title: Shade Start
128.description: If enabled, the beam will fade towards the start.

256.title: Shade End
256.description: If enabled, the beam will fade towards the end. Will not function if "Shade Start" is enabled.

skin.title: Beverage Type
skin.description: Sets the texture of the cans. You can set other than these to use if you're using a custom model, these are the skin index.

m_UseType.title: Use Type
m_UseType.description: Anything else will be count as USE_UNKNOWN and in fact, work the same as USE_UNSET

-1.title: USE_UNSET (default)
-1.description: This is the value by default and will have no effect at all in how entities normaly behave.

2.title: USE_SET
2.description: Does use of m_UseValue, See how it affects each entity.

4.title: USE_KILL
4.description: Remove the entity from the world.

5.title: USE_SAME
5.description: Sends the same USE_TYPE that this entity receive.

6.title: USE_OPPOSITE
6.description: Sends the opposite USE_TYPE that this entity receive, Swaps USE_ON <-> USE_OFF, If anything else then USE_TOGGLE is sent

7.title: USE_TOUCH
7.description: Calls the Touch function, the activator will be passed as the toucher.

8.title: USE_LOCK
8.description: Locks the entity. Does use of m_UseValue

9.title: USE_UNLOCK
9.description: Un-Locks the entity. Does use of m_UseValue

custom_hull_min.title: Custom Min hullsize (X Y Z)
custom_hull_min.description: Custom minimum hull size. This overrides the size specified by models and entities.

custom_hull_max.title: Custom Max hullsize (X Y Z)
custom_hull_max.description: Custom maximum hull size. This overrides the size specified by models and entities.

2048.title: Only in Singleplayer
2048.description: This entity won't spawn at all in multi-player

targetname.title: Name
targetname.description: Name is a general attribute used to identify entities

sequence.title: Animation Sequence (editor)
sequence.description: Sequence to display in Jackhammer. This does not affect gameplay.

health.title: Volume (10 = loudest)
health.description: Sets the volume of the sound. Scales from 0 (completely silent) to 10 (maximum, default). (If using dynamics attributes below, this is the maximum volume the sound will reach.)
